title: Subitem inherit column values from Parent
excerpt:
Simple request that I think would be very useful- Would love the ability to grab column values from a sub items parent in the sub item itself, primarily for function column usage. 
I’d like to be able to do calculations …

posts.author: Skatcher (Sean Katcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Simple request that I think would be very useful- Would love the ability to grab column values from a sub items parent in the sub item itself, primarily for function column usage.

I’d like to be able to do calculations on values in specific sub items using a value set in the parent item. For example- Using parent items in a group to be POs and sub items of each PO to be billing points/line items. If I could communicate between sub item and parent item, I could fill out price points in columns per PO and then calculate totals based off of each sub item.

PO A bills at $1.00 per foot and has 5 sub items which refer to 5 different footage orders to invoice
PO B Bills at $1.50 per foot and has 3 sub items which refer to 3 different footage orders to invoice

If I could communicate between sub items and the parent item, I could input the price point in the parent item and use a formula to calculate total charges for each sub item based on price point set in the parent. Then I could relay that value summary back to the parent and determine if a PO is running low or not.

We have tried work around such as mirroring columns from the same board, but these don’t allow you to set defaults or anything, so unfortunately its just another column the user has to click. Plus mirroring a column from the same board seems kind of clunky.

posts.author: devitus (Vitaly Dmitriev)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this. This is currently preventing the whole team from using Monday.com as an OMS, where we have to have subitems inherit values, such as client, marketplace, currency and fee % from the parent item, otherwise it defeats the purpose.

I know this is marked “Highly Requested” and in Backlog with no ETA, but it’d be great to be able to escalate this to a higher priority level.

posts.author: elena (elena)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely and please in the automatation “create a subitem” include the option of having available the fields of the parent to insert in any column in the subitem.

posts.author: bcoon (Brian Coon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! Please add this feature. That would enable our team to do so much more! We are working on a product order system and what to be able to store past orders for an item in the subitems, so whenever the item’s status turns to ‘Ordered’, a subitem is created storing the quantity and price. This helps us to know how much we are spending.

Thank you!

Brian;

posts.author: cerkezi (smile)
posts.cookedHtml: This function definetly must be in monday !!

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: With all the big fund raising and share prices going up, you’d think some budget would be thrown into sorting all the subitem issue out. Been too long. It’s end of q2 yet features for q2 are still not even in Beta

posts.author: maxim (Maxim Krušina)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, we really neeed this.
And please, the same for placeholders in Emails and Activities - we need to use placeholders for parent item’s column values.

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml: This is another thing what I am looking for as a work around for calculating subitem column values based on parent item column values with a formula.

posts.author: DMarken (Danny)
posts.cookedHtml: I like the ability to make it independent, but I would love to be able to set the default to be the same so then we can change them later.

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I am using automations to create subitems as our items move from one stage to the other. We have also added a deadline column to the subitems on our board which allows them to show up in my week. These are great features and have helped our team track all the different tasks that are assigned to them. But since a lot of our subitems have the same name (because they are autogenerated) it makes it difficult to differentiate in my week which item these subs are related to.

I tried setting the subitem name in automation like this : “subitem name - {pulse.name}” but it didn’t recognize the variable and just named the subitem exactly like I had typed it out.

Is there any way to support the pulse variables as part of the naming for subitems during automatic creation?

Thanks

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Im fully support this. We need this feature too

posts.author: z_kolbeck (Zeina)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree. the pulse variables in automations for both items and subitems would be very useful!

posts.author: adinap (Adina Pierce)
posts.cookedHtml: I came here to see if this feature existed. This would be a big improvement!

posts.author: maryt (Mary)
posts.cookedHtml: I would really love for this feature to be available. It makes assigning subitems on ‘My Work’ easier to handle and trace.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also looking for this feature.

posts.author: stick_counter (Zack Fry)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Huge Need! Seems intuitive, for our uses. Not sure about everybody else.

posts.author: sargeduke (Luke Maslow)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please! This seems like it should be table steaks. I would expect the functionality to be able to go two-way: parent to child and vice versa with an ability to determine which you want in the settings.

posts.author: JustAnotherPM (Stuart Hooker)
posts.cookedHtml: The roadmap for '23 suggests sub-items will soon be able to inherit columns from the parent item, the next step has to be a tick-box that allows you to inherit the current value. The ultimate goal would be to have complete alignment i.e. an automation that would allow you to synch date/status/item name to sub-item once an item is either created or changed.

posts.author: pettravel (Susan Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to inherit columns from the parent item is now available, but the value of the parent cell needs to populate in the sub item cell as well. The value in the sub item cell should update if the cell value in the parent line changes. This would be a great time saver.

posts.author: madalyn (Madalyn Timchak)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this! I need to be able to use a value from a parent item in a formula of a subitem.

Monday Experts- Do we have any idea if this is coming down the pipeline?

posts.author: jessi (Jessi A)
posts.cookedHtml: I get around this by linking each subitem to its parent item and mirroring columns. However, when an item with subitems is duplicated, the subitems link to the original item instead of their new (duplicated) parent item. The links have to be updated manually and it’s painful.

posts.author: SailorsMoon (AL)
posts.cookedHtml:
Absolutely what I am waiting for, too.

This would be very (very!) useful for using pivot tables as well, because then you can analyze sub-items combined with data of their parent items.

posts.author: SailorsMoon (AL)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you please describe in short how to do this? I could not find documentation for this. Thank you!

posts.author: pettravel (Susan Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately, we have not heard that this feature is available yet. The issue with mirroring is that the subitem must be manually assigned to a parent item and can be assigned to any parent item. This is a manual transaction which must be done individually.

posts.author: SailorsMoon (AL)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, so it is not really what we finally want. We have tons of Items/sub-items, manually connecting them would be also not suitable as a workaround…

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all,

We developed an app that solves this exact pain point of subitems inherting column value from parent.

On creation - Populate item’s data into its subitems in a seamless way

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.31.152226×400 30.1 KB

On change - Being able to set dependency between a subitem to an item

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.30.162106×432 27.6 KB

There are also more advanced options with offset of dates -

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.36.032138×644 42.2 KB

It’s brand new (released last week) and we’re going to keep on adding new capabilities.

You can check it out here - https://monday.com/marketplace/10000287

Hope that helps!

posts.author: JemmaHoyle (Jemma Hoyle)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this too!

posts.author: HaikuStasi (Adam Lundstrom)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think the inheritance automation is a step in the right direction. But I am using a template item with pre-configured subitems as a template for new board items. I would like to see an inheritance formula that allows me to append data from a subitem column to inherited data from a parent item column, e.g.:
Parent item name - “DALE1-EA76708”
Subitem name - “EA Engineering Authorization”
Formula: CONCATENATE({}, LEFT({Subitem}, 2))
Output - “DALE1-EA76708EA”

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this is needed for deadlines. When an subitem is created, we need to be able to automatically assign a deadline based on the deadline of the main item.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jstokes

We developed an app called subitem automations that’s solves exactly that (and a bunch of other subitem automations). See an example →

Screenshot 2024-02-22 at 21.37.101974×292 16.7 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: Yup!! I would LOVE this. Right now we are doing tonnes of manual updates to subitems to match the Parent item to track quarterly goals and Sprints!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. How do I implement this inheritance?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Regan & @dk_NFroehlich

You can go to the marketplace and install the app called subitem automations . Once installed just go to the integrations, select the app and use this automation →

Screenshot 2024-03-21 at 16.04.512196×488 28.2 KB

Hope that helps!

posts.author: dylman3000 (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d love this, in my use case I would prefer it to be calculated, eg. Parent Priority value automatically inherits the highest value within the sub-items Priority value. So at the top level it is always showing the priority value of the most important sub-item.

posts.author: dylman3000 (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: As a work around I implemented this concept. It works OK, but not robust enough. For example I want my parent item’s priority to always show the highest priority value within a sub-item. My automation works when increasing the priority of an item, but not when decreasing the priority. If I automate the downward change in priority that will work too, but will override other sub-items with the higher priority if they were tagged high priority first. A more calculated approach is needed here.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The app Rollup Subitems (see monday.com: Apps Marketplace) can take the highest value of a number column in the subitems and put this in a number column in the parent items. Changes made to the column in the subitems will recalculate the highest number.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I think I do understand what you meant. There is an option to copy a column to subitems. But this only copies settings and description.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I have this app installed unfortunately - as I have stated in many places - I do not have the budget nor do I want to pay further subscriptions. The free plans never cover our needs as we have more than 20 people working in extremely complex boards.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the reply @dk_NFroehlich, perfectly understanble. Sorry I can’t offer any further assistance!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

I just spoke with the boards team and the ability for subitems to inherit values from parent items across various column types is not currently on the roadmap for this year. Our boards team is currently focusing on finishing the development of other highly requested board features, such as the ability to reorder views, wrap text, and undo capabilities. Once our team finishes working on these features, they will look into new features to add to upcoming roadmaps, so keep sharing your ideas!

posts.author: Leigh_73 (Leigh Skilton)
posts.cookedHtml: Ive added my vote for this feature

posts.author: ed.cupaioli (Ed)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m coming from Hive, Asana, Linear, and Jira and I’ve never experienced a UI so unintuitive. Why wouldn’t this be standard? What is gained from not having this? I don’t want to do an automation for every feature that should come standard. A spreadsheet is better than this.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Until this becomes a native feature in monday, you might find our Subitems Pro Automations app helpful! It includes recipes to copy column value from parent to all subitems:

image422×329 12.2 KB

image421×330 12.1 KB

App pricing is based solely on the number of seats, the usage is unlimited.

Got questions or need a custom automation? We’re happy to help — just reach out!

posts.author: ItamarY13 (Itamar Y.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! This is possible with Autoboost.

It allows using recipes and apply custom formulas - such as in this case - I am using a parent Status column called “Type” and the subitems are populated with the value I choose from the parent item.

Check it out here

Screenshot 2025-08-17 at 16.00.38985×502 30.4 KB

posts.author: megseegmiller (Meg Seegmiller)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature in 2026? Even the ability to create an automation that allows setting subitem values based on the parent would be super helpful.

posts.author: mia-cxmonday (Mia Leath)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi megseegmiller and everyone,

Mia here from the monday.com team. I’m stepping in to let you know that we hear your votes and comments loud and clear!

While this specific feature is currently not-on-roadmap as we focus on core platform updates, please know that high-value requests like subitem inheritance are always part of our product team’s long-term planning discussions.

I truly appreciate you keeping this topic active and sharing how much this would impact your workflow. I’m keeping a close eye on this and hope to share a more positive update in the future!
